target.title: fix(install): validate downloaded scripts before execution
target.body:
## Problem

`run_remote_bash` in `scripts/install.sh` (line 69) downloads a script via `download_file` and executes it with `/bin/bash "$tmp"` without any validation. While HTTPS/TLS1.2 protects the transport, truncated downloads, HTML error pages (e.g., CDN 403 responses), and empty responses would be silently executed as bash scripts.

The only call site downloads the Homebrew installer from `raw.githubusercontent.com`. Contrast with `bootstrap_gum_temp` (lines 187-197) which verifies downloaded content with SHA256 checksums.

## Fix

Add structural validation before execution:
1. Reject empty files (`[[ ! -s "$tmp" ]]`)
2. Reject files that don't start with a shebang (`#!`)

A full SHA256 checksum pin is impractical because the Homebrew installer updates frequently and a pinned hash would break the installer on every Homebrew release.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `run_remote_bash()` executed downloaded content with `/bin/bash` unconditionally. Truncated downloads, HTML error pages, JSON errors, and binary blobs would all be piped to bash without any check. The fix adds shebang and non-empty validation before execution.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), bash 5.2.21, OpenClaw source at `/tmp/fix-82955` branched from current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Run the included red-green test harness
cd /tmp/fix-82955
bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh

# 2. Test the actual install.sh validation function directly
bash -c '
source <(sed -n "/^run_remote_bash/,/^}/p" scripts/install.sh)
mktempfile() { mktemp; }
download_file() { cp "$1" "$2"; }
ui_error() { echo "ERROR: $*" >&2; }

echo "<html><body>404 Not Found</body></html>" > /tmp/fake-download.html
echo "Testing validation against HTML error page:"
run_remote_bash /tmp/fake-download.html
echo "Exit code: $?"
'
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

### Live validation test harness (red-green)

Ran the included `scripts/test-install-validation.sh` which extracts the exact validation logic from the patched `install.sh` and tests it against 6 input types:

```console
$ bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh

── GREEN: validation rejects bad downloads ──
  ✓ PASS  empty file  → rejected
  ✓ PASS  HTML error   → rejected
  ✓ PASS  JSON error   → rejected
  ✓ PASS  binary blob  → rejected
  ✓ PASS  valid script (#!bash)     → accepted
  ✓ PASS  valid script (#!env bash) → accepted

── RED: no validation lets bad downloads through ──
  ✓ PASS  empty file  → accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  ✓ PASS  HTML error   → accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  ✓ PASS  JSON error   → accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  ✓ PASS  binary blob  → accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)

── Error messages shown to the user ──
--- empty file ---
✗ Downloaded script is empty: https://example.com/missing.sh
--- HTML error page ---
✗ Downloaded file does not look like a shell script (no shebang): https://raw.githubusercontent.com/example/install.sh
✗ First line: <html>
--- JSON error ---
✗ Downloaded file does not look like a shell script (no shebang): https://api.example.com/script
✗ First line: {"error":"not_found","message":"The requested resource does not exist","status":
--- binary blob ---
✗ Downloaded file does not look like a shell script (no shebang): https://example.com/corrupted.sh
✗ First line: ;;U…IJ……YZV………#… Is……J……………>………J…,……̧………`n………',

Results: 10 passed, 0 failed
All checks passed — validation works as intended.
```

### Direct install.sh function test

Sourced the actual `run_remote_bash` function from the patched `install.sh` and ran it against an HTML error page:

```console
$ bash -c 'source <(sed -n "/^run_remote_bash/,/^}/p" scripts/install.sh) ...'
Testing validation against HTML error page:
ERROR: Downloaded file does not look like a shell script (no shebang): /tmp/fake-download.html
ERROR: First line: <html><body>404 Not Found</body></html>
Exit code: 1
```

The validation correctly rejects the HTML page with a clear diagnostic message showing the URL and first line of content.

Observed result after fix: Without validation (old code), all 4 bad inputs (empty, HTML, JSON, binary) are silently accepted and would be executed by bash. With validation (this PR), all 4 are rejected with clear error messages. Valid scripts with `#!` shebangs pass through. The validation function works correctly when sourced directly from the patched `install.sh`.

What was not tested: Live download from a CDN returning an error page. The structural validation is tested offline against realistic inputs (HTML 404, JSON error, binary blob, empty file). A live CDN test would require deliberately triggering a server error, which is not practical in CI.
target.author: sebtardif
